request.question: Justin Hurwitz is best known for scoring an American musical romantic comedy-drama film that stars who as a jazz pianist?

title: Gil Evans
Blue Hawaii
La La Land (film)
Justin Hurwitz
Gigi (Hank Jones album)
Iiro Rantala
From Justin to Kelly
City of Stars
List of accolades received by La La Land
High School Musical (franchise)
sentences: Ian Ernest Gilmore "Gil" Evans (born Green; May 13, 1912 – March 20, 1988) was a Canadian jazz pianist, arranger, composer and bandleader.
He is widely recognized as one of the greatest orchestrators in jazz, playing an important role in the development of cool jazz, modal jazz, free jazz and jazz fusion.
He is best known for his acclaimed collaborations with Miles Davis.
Blue Hawaii is a 1961 American musical romantic comedy film set in the state of Hawaii and starring Elvis Presley.
The screenplay by Hal Kanter was nominated by the Writers Guild of America in 1962 in the category of Best Written American Musical.

The movie opened at no. 2 in box office receipts for that week and despite mixed reviews from critics, finished as the 10th top-grossing movie of 1961 and 14th for 1962 on the "Variety" national box office survey, earning $5 million.
The film won a fourth place prize Laurel Award in the category of Top Musical of 1961.
La La Land is a 2016 American musical romantic comedy-drama film written and directed by Damien Chazelle.
It stars Ryan Gosling as a jazz pianist and Emma Stone as an aspiring actress, who meet and fall in love in Los Angeles while pursuing their dreams.
The film's title refers simultaneously to music, the city of Los Angeles, and to the idiom for being out of touch with reality.
Justin Hurwitz (born 22 January 1985) is an American film composer and a television writer.
He is best known for scoring the film "La La Land".
He is a frequent collaborator with director Damien Chazelle and also scored Chazelle's two other features, "Guy and Madeline on a Park Bench" and "Whiplash".

Gigi (full title Hank Jones Swings Songs from Lerner and Loewe's Gigi ) is an album by American jazz pianist Hank Jones featuring jazz adaptations of tunes from Alan Jay Lerner and Frederick Loewe's musical romantic comedy film "Gigi" recorded in 1958 and released on the Golden Crest label.
Iiro Rantala (born 1970, in Helsinki) is a Finnish jazz pianist.
He studied piano in the jazz department of Sibelius Academy and classical piano at the Manhattan School of Music.
He is one of the best known Finnish jazz pianists, both in Finland and abroad.
Rantala is a pianist and composer with Trio Töykeät, a Finnish jazz trio.
In addition to jazz, he has composed some classical pieces, most notably the "Concerto for Piano and Concerto in G♯ΔA♭".
From Justin to Kelly is a 2003 American musical romantic comedy film written by Kim Fuller and directed by Robert Iscove.
The film, starring "American Idol" first season winner Kelly Clarkson and runner-up Justin Guarini, won the Golden Raspberry Award for Worst 'Musical' of Our First 25 Years in 2005.
This film is often regarded as one of the worst movies ever made.
"City of Stars" is a song performed by Ryan Gosling and Emma Stone from the film "La La Land" (2016).
The music of the song was composed by Justin Hurwitz while the lyrics were provided by Benj Pasek and Justin Paul.
The song won numerous awards, including the award for Best Original Song at both the 74th Golden Globe Awards and 89th Academy Awards.
"La La Land" is a 2016 American romantic musical comedy-drama film written and directed by Damien Chazelle.
Starring Ryan Gosling and Emma Stone, the film focuses on two young people struggling to make ends meet in Los Angeles, while pursuing their dreams as artists.
Justin Hurwitz composed the film's musical score, while Linus Sandgren was the cinematographer.
David and Sandy Reynolds-Wasco were responsible for the production design and Mary Zophres designed the costumes.
The "High School Musical" franchise consists of four Disney musical romantic comedy-drama films directed by Kenny Ortega and created by Peter Barsocchini.
It stars Zac Efron, Vanessa Hudgens, Ashley Tisdale, Lucas Grabeel, Corbin Bleu, and Monique Coleman.
The original film was released simply as a Disney Channel Original Movie, but after its huge success, it was followed by a television sequel.
The received a theatrical release.
Tisdale and Grabeel reprised their roles as Sharpay and Ryan Evans in a spin-off on the series titled "Sharpay's Fabulous Adventure", it was a direct to video film.
Numerous international versions have been created, plus stage musicals and video games have been released, among other things.
On March 1, 2016, a fourth installment was announced.
The films have developed a cult following.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Role: This agent summarizes first-hop evidence and exposes the bridge entity, any missing fact(s), ambiguity, and concrete next-hop retrieval directions. Do not attempt to produce the final answer—the summary should tell the next agent exactly what to fetch to finish the multi-hop chain.

Output requirements (single `summary` value only):
- Start the summary with the header: First-hop findings:
- Include these labeled parts (use a short bullet list or 2–4 concise sentences; keep overall output brief):
  1) Evidence found: For each person/entity named in the question, list which passage(s) mention matching candidate pages/titles and quote any explicit fact tokens found there (names, birth years, publication years, short parenthetical notes). Keep quoted fragments <=15 words and cite the passage title/label for each fact.
  2) Ambiguities: If a name yields multiple candidate pages or variants in the passages, explicitly enumerate those candidate titles/labels as they appear; if an expected page/variant is missing, note that too.
  3) Bridge entity / Missing fact: Explicitly name the bridge entity exactly as it appears in the passages (the person, work, or organization that links to the next hop) and state the precise missing fact needed to answer the question (e.g., a birth date, founding year, canonical series ordering).
  4) Retrieval direction: Provide 2–4 concrete, prioritized next-hop targets (specific page titles or disambiguation pages to fetch, and/or exact sections to check such as lede/infobox/Career/History). Include short example search keywords or one-line instructions (e.g., "open '<Name>' page and check lede/infobox and 'Career' section for 'Italian champion' year").
- End with a brief explicit note that the final answer is deferred until those pages are retrieved.

Hard constraints (do not violate):
- Do not provide a final comparison or answer here. Do not guess or invent facts not present in the provided passages or introduce outside knowledge.
- Do not assume which candidate is intended when multiple candidates exist.
- Avoid long verbatim copying of passages (>15 words).
- Preserve concise factual phrasing and verbatim tokens that appear in the passages (names, dates, page titles) when reporting evidence.

Keep the summary concise and focused—just the first-hop findings needed to guide the next retrieval hop.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Output contract:
- Produce exactly one field named `query` whose value is a single short retrieval query string (one line, no surrounding explanation or extra fields).
- Do not answer, summarize, or explain anything in `query`. Do not include fallback instructions or user-facing requests for more info in this field.

Construction rules (apply the most specific rule that fits the inputs):
1) Linking two named works: If `question` or `summary_1` names two or more specific works (songs, tracks, titles) and the missing evidence is the bridge between them, emit a concise interrogative that names both works and requests the linking evidence (artist / album / recording). Keep it short (about ≤ 12 words). Example form: "Which artist or album recorded both 'Work A' and 'Work B'?"
2) Missing album artist/genre: If `summary_1` indicates the album title is present but the artist or genre is unknown, emit a compact search string of the form: <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
   - No punctuation, no question phrasing, no extra words. Example: "Levitate album artist genre"
3) Sports season-year lookups: If the `question` contains a specific season-year token and the missing bridge is a league-season page, emit a short query that includes the year token, a league identifier (e.g., "NHL" or "National Hockey League"), and a cue such as "regular season" or "season count". Keep it concise (≤ ~10 tokens).
4) Default: Otherwise, produce one concise search-style query (≤ ~12 words) that uses visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` targeted at retrieving the missing supporting evidence (artist, album, recording, genre, season page, etc.).

General guidance:
- Keep queries compact; avoid lists, long page titles, or copying large spans from hidden targets.
- Do not invent facts or candidate answers; use only visible tokens or neutral retrieval cues.
- Prefer a short search string; use an interrogative only for the two-works linking case in rule 1.
- The goal is to maximize recovery of the missing bridge evidence for the downstream retriever.

Return only the single `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Primary function: integrate the newly retrieved `passages` (second-hop evidence) with the provided `context` (first-hop) so the output is a grounded post-retrieval state that preserves the facts the final-answer generator needs. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when evidence is insufficient.

Behavior rules (minimal, general):
1) Preserve concise-answer behavior. If `context` already contains a concise direct answer (a short sentence or phrase), keep that text exactly as the first sentence of `summary`, unless it contradicts the `passages`.
2) When `passages` supply additional, directly supported facts about entities named in the `question` or `context`, append up to one brief supporting sentence per distinct named entity (keep total `summary` to 1–3 sentences, preferably 1–2). Each supporting sentence should: start with the entity's full name as shown in the passages, include a short paraphrased attribution drawn only from the passages (no more than ~10–12 words) such as nationality, a notable achievement, a birth date, or a current affiliation, and avoid inventing or inferring facts not present in the inputs.
3) When an object/entity page (for example, a car page, group page, or similar) lists associated people and a separate passage provides a biography for one of those listed people, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary: (a) first sentence: name the people as listed on the object page (e.g., "driven by X and Y" or "member of Group Z"); (b) optional second short clause/sentence: paraphrase key biographical facts from the person's passage (only facts present there — e.g., nationality, notable wins/awards, birth date, current team/affiliation). Keep wording compact and factual.
4) Ground everything in the provided `passages` and `context`. Do not add new claims, dates, roles, or affiliations not explicitly supported. Avoid long verbatim quotations from the passages; you may paraphrase short phrases (≤10 words) only if they appear in the sources, and you may copy short strings such as names or dates exactly as shown.
5) If the passages do not provide enough evidence to resolve the bridge, state that the passages do not specify the requested fact while still listing any supported names or facts. Do not guess or fabricate missing information.
6) Output contract: return only the field `summary` containing 1–3 concise sentences (preferably 1–2) that integrate the first-hop `context` and second-hop `passages` according to the rules above. Do not add commentary, answer-labels, or extraneous text.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, and `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only the visible summaries (summary_1 and summary_2) together with the question as your evidence. Do not introduce external facts, sources, or claims not present in those summaries.
- Treat summary_2 (the later, integrated post-retrieval state) as primary evidence while still considering summary_1. Do not assume the final state is unresolved simply because summary_1 describes an earlier unresolved bridge; rely on the combined visible summaries to reach a conclusion.
- If summary_1 and summary_2 both clearly and unambiguously support the same binary (yes/no) conclusion to the question, then the value of the `answer` field must be exactly one lowercase token: either "yes" (if both summaries affirm the fact) or "no" (if both summaries deny the fact). The `answer` field must contain only that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no capitalization, no explanation, no additional words).
- If the summaries conflict, are ambiguous, or do not together provide a clear binary yes/no verdict, produce a concise answer string in the `answer` field that best reflects what the summaries support. This concise answer may be a short phrase or sentence, but must still be supported solely by the summaries and must avoid adding new facts.
- Always minimize verbosity: prefer the single-token binary response when the summaries unambiguously agree; otherwise keep the answer brief and directly tied to the visible summaries.

Produce only the `answer` field as output.

response.hop1_query: Justin Hurwitz is best known for scoring an American musical romantic comedy-drama film that stars who as a jazz pianist?
hop1_docs: List of accolades received by La La Land | "La La Land" is a 2016 American romantic musical comedy-drama film written and directed by Damien Chazelle.  Starring Ryan Gosling and Emma Stone, the film focuses on two young people struggling to make ends meet in Los Angeles, while pursuing their dreams as artists.  Justin Hurwitz composed the film's musical score, while Linus Sandgren was the cinematographer.  David and Sandy Reynolds-Wasco were responsible for the production design and Mary Zophres designed the costumes.

Justin Hurwitz | Justin Hurwitz (born 22 January 1985) is an American film composer and a television writer.  He is best known for scoring the film "La La Land".  He is a frequent collaborator with director Damien Chazelle and also scored Chazelle's two other features, "Guy and Madeline on a Park Bench" and "Whiplash".

La La Land (film) | La La Land is a 2016 American musical romantic comedy-drama film written and directed by Damien Chazelle.  It stars Ryan Gosling as a jazz pianist and Emma Stone as an aspiring actress, who meet and fall in love in Los Angeles while pursuing their dreams.  The film's title refers simultaneously to music, the city of Los Angeles, and to the idiom for being out of touch with reality.

The Fabulous Baker Boys | The Fabulous Baker Boys is a 1989 American romantic comedy-drama musical film written and directed by Steve Kloves, and starring real life brothers Jeff Bridges and Beau Bridges as two brothers struggling to make a living as lounge jazz pianists in Seattle.  In desperation, they take on a female singer, Michelle Pfeiffer, who revitalizes their careers, causing the brothers to re-examine their relationship with each other and with their music.
Upstage (film) | Upstage (also known as The Mask of Comedy) is a 1926 American silent romantic drama film directed by Monta Bell, starring Norma Shearer and New York musical comedy star Oscar Shaw.

From Justin to Kelly | From Justin to Kelly is a 2003 American musical romantic comedy film written by Kim Fuller and directed by Robert Iscove.  The film, starring "American Idol" first season winner Kelly Clarkson and runner-up Justin Guarini, won the Golden Raspberry Award for Worst 'Musical' of Our First 25 Years in 2005.  This film is often regarded as one of the worst movies ever made.

List of Amy Adams performances | Amy Adams is an American actress who made her film debut in the 1999 black comedy "Drop Dead Gorgeous".  She went on to guest star in a variety of television shows, including "That '70s Show", "Charmed", "Buffy the Vampire Slayer", and "The Office", and also appeared in minor film roles.  In 2002, she had her first major role in Steven Spielberg's biographical crime drama "Catch Me If You Can".  However, the film did not launch her career as Spielberg had hoped.  Three years later, she made the breakthrough with the comedy-drama "Junebug" (2005), for which she received her first Academy Award for Best Supporting Actress nomination.  Adams also appeared in the romantic comedy "The Wedding Date" that same year.  In 2007, she starred in the Disney romantic comedy "Enchanted", for which she won the Saturn Award for Best Actress, and was nominated for her first Golden Globe Award for Best Actress (Comedy or Musical).
hop1_titles: List of accolades received by La La Land
response.summary_1:
First-hop findings:
- Evidence found:
  - Justin Hurwitz: passage [2] — quoted: "best known for scoring the film 'La La Land'." (passage 2)
  - La La Land cast/role: passage [3] — quoted: "It stars Ryan Gosling as a jazz pianist" (passage 3); also passage [1] — quoted: "Starring Ryan Gosling and Emma Stone" (passage 1).
- Ambiguities:
  - None in the provided passages — "La La Land" and "Justin Hurwitz" each appear with single matching entries; no alternative film candidates shown.
- Bridge entity / Missing fact:
  - Bridge entity: "La La Land" (film).
  - Missing fact needed to answer the question precisely: the actor's name who stars as the jazz pianist in La La Land (i.e., confirmation of the credited performer/role).
- Retrieval direction (prioritized):
  1) Open "La La Land (film)" page — check the lede/infobox and the "Cast" section for the credited actor who "stars ... as a jazz pianist" (search keywords: La La Land cast "jazz pianist", "It stars Ryan Gosling as a jazz pianist").
  2) Open "Justin Hurwitz" page — verify the lede states he scored La La Land (lede/infobox).
  3) Optionally open "Ryan Gosling" page — check lede/filmography or role list to confirm he played the jazz pianist (role name and credit).
Final answer is deferred until those pages/sections are retrieved and verified.
response.hop2_query: La La Land cast jazz pianist actor
hop2_docs: La La Land (film) | La La Land is a 2016 American musical romantic comedy-drama film written and directed by Damien Chazelle.  It stars Ryan Gosling as a jazz pianist and Emma Stone as an aspiring actress, who meet and fall in love in Los Angeles while pursuing their dreams.  The film's title refers simultaneously to music, the city of Los Angeles, and to the idiom for being out of touch with reality.

Jazz à la Bohemia | Jazz à la Bohemia (also released as Greenwich Village Jazz) is a live album by American jazz pianist Randy Weston's trio with saxophonist Cecil Payne which was recorded in 1956 at the Café Bohemia in Greenwich Village and released on the Riverside label.

La Vie de Bohème (album) | La Vie de Bohème is a studio album released by jazz pianist Dave Burrell.  The album is Burrell's take on the operatic adaptation of La Vie de Bohème by Giacomo Puccini, titled "La bohème".  The album has been called "a fine example of the similarities between the free jazz and classical worlds."  Though this is not a straight performance of the opera, each of the acts are represented with "a great deal of improvisation."

La Leyenda de La Hora | La Leyenda de La Hora (The Legend of the Hour) is a 1981 album by jazz pianist McCoy Tyner released on the Columbia label.  It features performances by Tyner with Paquito D'Rivera, Chico Freeman, Marcus Belgrave, Hubert Laws, Bobby Hutcherson and a string section.
Piano á la Mode | Piano á la Mode is an album by American jazz pianist Randy Weston which was recorded in 1957 and released on the Jubilee label.

The Nightingale casting controversy | In 2012, the La Jolla Playhouse generated nationwide controversy for the casting of a musical adaptation of a Hans Christian Andersen story "The Nightingale," set in ancient China.  The play was part of the La Jolla Playhouse's "Page to Stage" program, a workshop series in which unfinished new works are tested and altered during the show's run.  Only two Asian American actors were cast, and more than half the cast were Caucasian actors.  The Asian American Theatre Community expressed their indignity on this issue.

Llegó La India Via Eddie Palmieri | Llegó La India Via Eddie Palmieri ("La India Has Arrived Via Eddie Palmieri") is a salsa album by Puerto Rican-American singer La India in collaboration with pianist Eddie Palmieri.  It was released in the US by Soho Sounds in 1992 and features songs in Spanish and English.  The album was also released in the UK by Acid Jazz Records in 1993.
hop2_titles: Llegó La India Via Eddie Palmieri
